content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
• 1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Kristopher J. Kokotaylo 
Assistant City Attorney 
City of San Leandro 
555 12th Street, Suite 1500 
Oakland, CA 94607 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-17-173 
Dear Mr. Kokotaylo: 
August 4, 2017 
This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf San Leandro Vice Mayor Lee 
Thomas regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the .. Act").1 
Please note that we are only providing advice under the conflict of interest provisions of the 
Act and not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common law conflict of 
interest or Section 1090. 
QUESTION 
May the Vice Mayor appear before the City Council and/or the City Board of Zoning 
Adjustments (the .. Zoning Board") in connection with an appeal of his application to modify his 
fence? 
CONCLUSION 
Yes. The Vice Mayor may appear before the City Council and/or the City Board of Zoning 
Adjustments (the "Zoning Board") in connection with an appeal of his application to modify his 
fence so long as he appears as any other member of the general public to represent his own personal 
interests in his real property? 
FACTS 
San Leandro Zoning Code and Fence Modification Process. The City has a Zoning Code 
(the "Code") that generally governs pennissibld physical development within the City. Section 4-
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 ofTitle 2 of the California Code ofRegulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


FileNo. A-17-173 
Page No. 2 
1682 of the Code specifies the maximum pennissible height of a fence within various districts 
throughout the City. 
Section 4-1682 pennits the City's Zoning Enforcement Official to grant fence modifications 
to applicants upon certain findings to allow modifications to the maximum height, minimum 
setback, and material of construction for fences. 
Appeals of the decision of the Zoning Enforcement Official approving or denying a fence 
modification are heard by the Zoning Board. Decisions of the Board may be appealed to the City 
Council and the City Council's decision is final. 
Vice Mayor Thomas' Fence Modification Application. Vice Mayor Thomas owns residential 
property in the city with his spouse as sole owners. The Vice Mayor submitted an application for a 
fence modification. The Zoning Enforcement Official granted the fence modification for his 
residential property. Subsequently, a member of the public appealed the Zoning Enforcement 
Official's detennination to grant the fence modification and the appeal will be considered by the 
Board. The appeal is scheduled to be considered by the Board in September. 
ANALYSIS 
The Act's conflict-of-interest rules prohibit a public official from making, participating in 
making, or using his or her official position in any way to influence a governmental decision in 
which the official knows, or has reason to know, that he or she has a "financial interest." (Section 
87100.) A public official has a financial interest in a decision within the meaning of Section 87103 
if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect, distinguishable 
from its effect on the public generally, on the official, a member of his or her immediate family, or 
on any of the enumerated interests in Section 87103. 
Your question concerns the Vice Mayor's real property. (Section 87103(b).) We assume the 
value of the real property is $2,000 or more. You acknowledged that the Zoning Board's decision 
regarding the Vice Mayor's fence modification application will have a reasonably foreseeable 
material financial effect on Vice Mayor Thomas' interest in his real property. You ask whether the 
Vice Mayor may influence city agencies such as (the city council and zoning board) in connection 
with his fence modification application and ensuing appeal. 
Regulation 18704(c) provides that an official is using his or her official position to influence 
a decision if the official: 
"(I) Contacts or appears before any official in his or her agency or in an 
agency subject to the authority or budgetary control of his or her agency for the 
purpose of affecting a decisi9n; or 
"(2) Contacts or appears before any official in any other government 
agency for the purpose of affecting a decision, and the public official acts or 
purports to act within his or her authority or on behalf of his or her agency in 
making the contact." 


FileNo. A-17-173 
Page No. 3 
The existence of a conflict of interest would prohibit an official from influencing his own 
agency (the city council), absent an exception. (Regulation 18704(c)(l)) The Vice Mayor is not a 
member of the Zoning Board, however, the zoning board members are appointed by the City 
Council. Thus, pursuant to Regulation 18704(c){l), the Vice Mayor would also be prohibited from 
influencing the zoning board. 
However, as you note, Regulation l 8704(d)(2) provides an exception for an otherwise 
disqualified official to appear as a member of the general public. It provides, in pertinent part, that 
making, participating in, or influencing a governmental decision does not include: 
"An appearance by a public official as a member of the general public 
before an agency in the course of its prescribed governmental function if the 
official is appearing on matters related solely to the his or her personal interests, 
including interests in: 
"(A) Real property owned entirely by the official, members of his or her 
immediate family, or the official and members of his or her immediate family ... " 
This exception would permit the Vice Mayor to appear before both bodies. 
However, note that this exception is interpreted narrowly and care should be taken to clarify 
that the official is not acting in an official capacity. (McHuglr Advice Letter, No. I-98-324.) In 
addition, while the Vice Mayor may participate fully in the appeal in the same manner as any other 
member of the public, the Vice Mayor may not contact, any member of the City Council, Zoning 
Board, or City staff related to the fence modification application, outside the appeal process. 
Moreover, as you noted, should the appeal reach the city council, the Vice Mayor would 
have additional responsibilities before appearing to represent his personal interests.2 When a public 
official holds an office specified in Section 87200 (such as the Vice Mayor), the official must: 
(1) Immediately prior to the discussion of the item, orally identify each type of interest 
involved in the decision as well as details of the interest on the record of the meeting; 
(2) Recuse himself or herself; and 
(3) Leave the room for the duration of the discussion and/or vote on the item. (Section 
87105.) 
Notwithstanding this requirement, the Vice Mayor may remain in the room and speak on the 
issue during the time that the general public speaks on the issue, as discussed above. 
2 These requirements would not apply to the hearing before the Zoning Board level. 


FileNo. A-17-173 
Page No. 4 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
JWW:jgl 
Sincerely, 
Jack C. Woodside 
General Counsel 
tant General Counsel 
Division

Adjustments (the .. Zoning Board") in connection with an appeal of his application to modify his 
fence?

Adjustments (the "Zoning Board") in connection with an appeal of his application to modify his 
fence so long as he appears as any other member of the general public to represent his own personal 
interests in his real property?

FileNo. A-17-173
